## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-23 20:14:30
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Goal
Fix `code/twocaptcha/solver.py` to make all test suite tests pass by addressing three issues.

## Proposed Approach

### Requirement 1 — Default softId=4580
- In `TwoCaptcha.__init__`, change `self.soft_id = softId` to `self.soft_id = softId if softId is not None else 4580`
- This ensures every API call includes `soft_id=4580` when the caller omits `softId` or passes `softId=None`
- No change needed to `default_params` — the existing `if soft_id:` check already works once the value is non-None/truthy

### Requirement 2 — Add 9 missing captcha methods
Add these methods to `TwoCaptcha`:
- `tencent(self, app_id, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='tencent', app_id=app_id, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `turnstile(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='turnstile', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `friendly_captcha(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='friendly_captcha', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `geetest_v4(self, captcha_id, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='geetest_v4', captcha_id=captcha_id, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `mtcaptcha(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='mt_captcha', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `atb_captcha(self, app_id, api_server, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='atb_captcha', app_id=app_id, api_server=api_server, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `lemin(self, captcha_id, div_id, api_server, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='lemin', captcha_id=captcha_id, div_id=div_id, api_server=api_server, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `amazon_waf(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='amazon_waf', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)`
- `cutcaptcha(self, misery_key, apikey, url, **kwargs)` → `solve(method='cutcaptcha', misery_key=misery_key, api_key=apikey, url=url, **kwargs)`

### Requirement 3 — Add `datas` → `data-s` rename
- In `rename_params`, add `'datas': 'data-s'` to the `replace` dict

## Expected Deliverables
- Modified `code/twocaptcha/solver.py` with all three fixes applied
- All 24+ tests passing

## Test Plan
- Test command: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- Expected coverage: all test files in `tests/` directory
- No test files will be modified

## Quality Gate
No lint/format tooling detected in the project; correctness verified by test suite.

requirements.description:
The TwoCaptcha constructor accepts softId=None as its default, and
      default_params only includes softId in the outgoing API request when the
      value is truthy (if soft_id: params.update(...)). As a result, no SDK
      partner ID is ever sent when the caller omits softId, but every test in
      the suite asserts that soft_id=4580 appears in every API call. This is
      the single root cause that makes all 15 non-AttributeError tests fail.
requirements.contract:
When no softId argument is supplied by the caller, the TwoCaptcha instance
      must use a hardcoded default SDK partner value of 4580. This default must
      be applied at construction time (i.e., self.soft_id should be 4580 when
      softId is None or omitted), so that default_params automatically includes
      soft_id=4580 in every outgoing request. When the caller explicitly passes
      softId=None the default should still apply; when the caller passes a
      non-None integer or string the supplied value must be used instead.
      No other behaviour of the constructor or default_params should change.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
The following tests must pass after this change alone (i.e., independently
      of the other requirements):
        tests/test_normal.py::NormalTest::test_base64
        tests/test_text.py::TextTest::test_only_text
        tests/test_text.py::TextTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_capy.py::CapyTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_geetest.py::GeeTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_hcaptcha.py::HcaptchaTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_keycaptcha.py::KeyCaptchaTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_grid.py::GridTest::test_base64_param
        tests/test_coordinates.py::CoordinatesTest::test_base64_param
        tests/canvas_test.py::CanvasTest::test_base64_param
        tests/test_canvas.py::CanvasTest::test_base64_param
        tests/test_funcaptcha.py::FuncaptchaTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_recaptcha.py::RecaptchaTest::test_v3

requirements.location: code/twocaptcha/solver.py — TwoCaptcha class (new methods to be added)
requirements.description:
Nine captcha-type methods are completely absent from the TwoCaptcha class,
      causing AttributeError when the tests call them:
        tencent, cutcaptcha, geetest_v4, atb_captcha, friendly_captcha,
        turnstile, lemin, amazon_waf, mtcaptcha.
      Each test passes typed keyword arguments and asserts that the resulting
      API call contains specific renamed/transformed keys, so the methods must
      not merely forward **kwargs blindly but must set the correct method string
      and apply any necessary parameter renaming before delegating to self.solve.
requirements.contract:
Each method must accept the parameters listed below and call self.solve
      with the exact outgoing keys shown. The url parameter in all methods must
      be passed to self.solve as the keyword url (which rename_params already
      converts to pageurl). Optional extra keyword arguments (**kwargs) must be
      forwarded unchanged.

      tencent(self, app_id, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='tencent', app_id=app_id, url=url, **kwargs)

      turnstile(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='turnstile', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)

      friendly_captcha(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='friendly_captcha', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)

      geetest_v4(self, captcha_id, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='geetest_v4', captcha_id=captcha_id, url=url, **kwargs)

      mtcaptcha(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='mt_captcha', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)

      atb_captcha(self, app_id, api_server, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='atb_captcha', app_id=app_id, api_server=api_server,
                url=url, **kwargs)

      lemin(self, captcha_id, div_id, api_server, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='lemin', captcha_id=captcha_id, div_id=div_id,
                api_server=api_server, url=url, **kwargs)

      amazon_waf(self, sitekey, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='amazon_waf', sitekey=sitekey, url=url, **kwargs)

      cutcaptcha(self, misery_key, apikey, url, **kwargs)
        → solve(method='cutcaptcha', misery_key=misery_key, api_key=apikey,
                url=url, **kwargs)
        Note: the caller passes the argument as apikey but the API requires the
        key api_key; the method must perform this single rename.

      All methods must return the result of self.solve.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
Combined with requirement 1, the following tests must pass:
        tests/test_tencent.py::TencentTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_turnstile.py::TurnstileTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_friendly_captcha.py::FriendlyCaptchaTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_geetest_v4.py::GeeTest_V4::test_all_params
        tests/test_mtcaptcha.py::MTCaptchaTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_atb_captcha.py::AtbCaptchaTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_lemin.py::LeminTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_amazon_waf.py::AmazonWAFTest::test_all_params
        tests/test_cutcaptcha.py::CutcaptchaTest::test_all_params

requirements.description:
The rename_params method maintains a replace mapping of camelCase/alias
      parameter names to the API field names. The recaptcha v2 test passes a
      parameter named datas (a Google reCAPTCHA data-s token), but the current
      replace dict has no entry for it. The API expects the field name data-s,
      so the value is forwarded under the wrong name datas, causing the
      assertion mismatch in test_recaptcha.py::RecaptchaTest::test_v2.
requirements.contract:
The replace mapping inside rename_params must include an entry that
      converts the caller-facing key datas to the outgoing API key data-s.
      All other entries in the mapping must remain unchanged.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
Combined with requirement 1, the following test must pass:
        tests/test_recaptcha.py::RecaptchaTest::test_v2
